# Facebook Post — OpenAI Winds Down Sora to Prioritize 'Spud' — Compute Economics Force Strategic Retreat
# Week: Week of 2026-03-23

# Status: Review before posting

---

OpenAI just shut down Sora — and every executive should take notes.

The AI video generator that once stunned the internet is gone. Not because it failed to impress. Because inference costs per generated minute were unsustainable. OpenAI is redirecting that compute to 'Spud,' a new model Sam Altman says will be ready in weeks.

This is the compute economics reckoning that has been building across the industry. When the company at the frontier of AI decides a product costs more to run than it delivers in value, that is a signal to every organization running AI in production.

Here is the executive question: how many AI features in your current stack would survive if you audited them purely on inference cost versus business value? Research from SXSW 2026 shows 67% of enterprise marketing budgets now include dedicated AI line items. But budget allocation and cost sustainability are two very different conversations.

The winners in 2026 will not be the companies with the most AI capabilities. They will be the ones whose AI capabilities are economically defensible.

## Fact-check notes
- OpenAI is winding down Sora, its AI video generator, to free up compute for its next major model 'Spud' — Source: Radical Data Science bulletin citing OpenAI announcements, 3/25/2026
- Sam Altman stated Spud will be ready in weeks — Source: Radical Data Science bulletin, 3/25/2026
- Digital Applied confirmed Sora shutdown was driven by unsustainable inference costs per generated minute — Source: digitalapplied.com March 2026 AI Roundup
